environment.repositoryStatus: Tracked implementation state was clean at the base commit; pre-existing untracked harness artifacts under .ide were untouched. The implementation commit contains exactly two authorized source additions.

task.userRequest: Implement exactly one new-subject pure-domain behavior: PublicKeyAlgorithm defines the complete case-sensitive CertificateProfile v1 vocabulary EC_P256 and RSA_3072 without declaration-order semantics. Trace to ADR 0001 and REQ-X509-PROFILE-INTEGRITY-001/011 without claiming YAML admission. Capture describe before the first subject edit, require JavaSpec-generated production creation, one meaningful RED, the smallest enum GREEN, and complete Java 21 verification.
requirements: ADR 0001 CertificateProfile v1 closed case-sensitive publicKeyAlgorithms vocabulary and unordered-set semantics
features/x509/certificate-profile-integrity.feature REQ-X509-PROFILE-INTEGRITY-001 (traceability only; complete scenario remains unimplemented)
features/x509/certificate-profile-integrity.feature REQ-X509-PROFILE-INTEGRITY-011 (traceability only; YAML admission and rejection remain unimplemented)

admission.reason: Complete sealed spec-driven RAW_EVENT_STREAM proves one ADR-authorized new-subject JavaSpec behavior with qualified requirement traceability, exact pre-baseline executable fingerprint, absent initial subject paths, describe before all subject edits, structural RED, first production creation by successful run --generate with exact generated-skeleton output, executable expectation RED, one bounded enum GREEN, order-insensitive exact vocabulary proof, selected/full 4/4 verification, zero pending/stubs, exact Java 21 public shape, Java 21 Maven and dependency success, exact two-file commit scope, matching hashes, schema/privacy checks, and independent behavior replay.

termination.summary: PublicKeyAlgorithm defines exactly EC_P256 and RSA_3072 as the complete case-sensitive v1 vocabulary without declaration-order semantics; all captured and independent behavior-verification gates pass.

knownIssues: The seal patchSha256 is null because both new source files were untracked at seal time; complete describe/generate/edit/final-file/hash events preserve their provenance and bytes, and the independently materialized commit patch matches them exactly.

The initial Maven baseline command ran under Java 25 despite a property override and failed on pre-existing JaCoCo compatibility; it was not used as baseline evidence. JavaSpec baseline was green before describe, and explicit Java 21 test/verify later passed.

The first final dry-run omitted the compiled specification classpath and skipped one example; it was not used as executable evidence, and the corrected classpath dry-run passed 1/1 with no pending generation work.

The launcher JAR at the selected path changed after sealing. Independent behavior replay passed with the later JAR but was not bit-identical executable replay; exact sealed pre-baseline provenance remains captured.
Mechanically and behaviorally validated candidate still requires independent approval for final CURATED_SFT.

sftEligibility.reason: RAW_EVENT_STREAM contains exactly one ADR-authorized new-subject behavior with qualified requirement traceability, complete describe/generate/RED/GREEN chronology, exact sealed executable provenance, bounded scope, matching commit/hash evidence, privacy sanitization, authoritative schema validation, and independent behavior replay.

resumeCapsule.currentBehavior: PublicKeyAlgorithm defines exactly the case-sensitive CertificateProfile v1 tokens EC_P256 and RSA_3072; the behavior assigns no declaration-order semantics and does not implement YAML admission.
resumeCapsule.lastVerifiedResult: Selected 1/1, full JavaSpec 4/4, zero pending/stubs, exact Java 21 enum shape, Java 21 Maven verify, and dependency checks passed; independently replayed behavior at the implementation commit.
resumeCapsule.lastFailureOrStop: Meaningful JavaSpec RED on the generated empty enum: expected the exact two-token set but got an empty set.
unresolvedWork: YAML admission and unknown/miscased token rejection remain unimplemented.
Parsing, set duplicates/order normalization, profile aggregate, JCS/hashing, manifests/audit, lifecycle, and issuance policy require separate authorized slices.
Independent approval is required before CURATED_SFT promotion.
Bit-identical replay would require recovery of the sealed SHA-256 0a6affa0... launcher JAR; behavior replay used the later JAR.
resumeCapsule.nextPermittedAction: Parent reviews and may commit dataset artifacts; any next domain change must be one separately authorized JavaSpec continuation slice, and javaspec describe must not be repeated for PublicKeyAlgorithm.
